name: F & S Transportation
districts_or_customers: School districts in York County PA
Carroll County MD (incl. Northeastern SD area)
recent_news: Site indicates ongoing operation with recent addition of wheelchair-accessible van rental; no M&A or litigation news surfaced.
fit_notes: Solid mid-size family-owned PA tuck-in with mixed school + charter + special-needs revenue and ~136 unit fleet in York/Adams counties; within Summit's PA expansion footprint and complementary to Fullington/McIlwain platform.

name: Randall Bus Svc
districts_or_customers: Northeast Florida and South Georgia charter customers
recent_news: 2021 News4Jax 'Positively Jax' feature highlighted Randall as Duval County's only African American-owned school bus operator and community charity work (shoe drive for students).
fit_notes: Multigenerational (80+ years) Duval County school-bus contractor with embedded DCPS relationship; small ~30-bus fleet limits standalone scale but offers minority-owned Jacksonville footprint and likely succession opportunity given founder family tenure.

name: Consolidated Bus Transit
districts_or_customers: NYC Department of Education (general & special education routes across all five boroughs)
recent_news: Company self-describes as a still-standing family-run business serving NYC 'for over 50 years' with ~25,000 students/day; no public M&A news surfaced.
fit_notes: Large NYC DOE contractor (25k+ daily students, Bronx + Brooklyn yards) — would be a platform-scale acquisition rather than tuck-in; private family ownership and 50-year tenure make it an attractive long-tail target if Summit pursues a NYC anchor, though revenue/headcount inputs in source data appear understated.

name: Total Transportation Corp
principals: Domenic Gatto family (former TTC owners, pre-2022)
districts_or_customers: NYC Department of Education (largest SPED/ADA provider)
recent_news: Acquired by First Student in summer 2022 (announced May 2, 2022; closed after regulatory clearance June 30, 2022); 15 locations and ~3,200 buses at deal close.
fit_notes: NOT actionable — owned by First Student since 2022. Disqualify from Summit tuck-in pipeline; useful as competitor benchmarking (multi-corp NYC roll-up archetype: 21st Ave, L&M, Quality, GVC II, Maggies, Brooklyn Transp., Bella, MAT PA, MPC NJ, Haverstraw).

name: Golden Ring Transportation Inc
principals: Yuriy Shabashkevich (President/Owner — Golden Ring Transportation Inc & affiliated Golden Ring Transit Inc)
districts_or_customers: NYC Department of Education (listed on NYC DOE bus company roster for school-age children)
Charter trips in 5 boroughs, Long Island, Westchester, Northern NJ
recent_news: No M&A or litigation news surfaced; FMCSA registration since June 1995 for the transportation entity and March 2015 for affiliated Golden Ring Transit Inc (charter authority).
fit_notes: Small (~35 unit) Brooklyn-based, owner-operator NYC DOE contractor with a charter sister entity; sub-scale on its own but a plausible add-on to a larger NYC platform — single-principal ownership (Shabashkevich) suggests potential succession window.
